reasoning: First iteration - generating initial query implementations. Learner has identified excellent optimization opportunities: Q6 can achieve 98% row group pruning due to lineitem being sorted by l_shipdate, Q1 has simple single-table aggregation with 5% row group pruning, and Q3 requires careful join ordering with smaller filtered customer table (3K rows) as first build side. All queries benefit from 50-75% I/O reduction via column projection.

query_guidance.Q1: Implement as: ScanWithDateFilter(l_shipdate <= '1998-09-02') → HashAgg → Sort. Use column projection to read only 7 of 16 columns (l_returnflag, l_linestatus, l_quantity, l_extendedprice, l_discount, l_tax, l_shipdate). Leverage row group pruning to skip last ~30 row groups (5% reduction). Use single-pass hash aggregation with low-cardinality GROUP BY (4 groups). Fuse expression evaluation (l_extendedprice * (1-l_discount) * (1+l_tax)) into aggregation loop using Arrow compute kernels to avoid materializing intermediate arrays.
query_guidance.Q3: Implement as: Scan(customer) + ScanWithDateFilter(orders, lineitem) → HashJoin(customer ⋈ orders) → HashJoin(result ⋈ lineitem) → HashAgg → Sort → Limit(10). Join order critical: (1) Build hash table on filtered customer (c_mktsegment='BUILDING', ~3K rows), probe with filtered orders (o_orderdate < '1995-03-15', ~6M rows); (2) Build on join result (~600K rows), probe with filtered lineitem (l_shipdate > '1995-03-15', ~36M rows). Use ScanWithDateFilter for lineitem to skip first 38% of row groups (1992-1995-03). Column projection: customer 2/8 cols, orders 4/9 cols, lineitem 4/16 cols. HashAgg on high-cardinality keys (~600K groups) then sort and limit to 10.
query_guidance.Q6: Implement as: ScanWithDateFilter(l_shipdate BETWEEN '1994-01-01' AND '1994-12-31') → Filter(l_discount BETWEEN 0.05 AND 0.07 AND l_quantity < 24) → Scalar Aggregate. This is the best-case scenario: row group pruning skips 98% of data (read only ~12 of 600 row groups covering 1994). Use column projection to read only 4 of 16 columns (l_shipdate, l_quantity, l_extendedprice, l_discount). Combine three predicates using arrow::compute::and_kleene into single filter mask. Fuse l_extendedprice * l_discount multiplication with sum aggregation to avoid materializing intermediate array. Expected result: scalar aggregate over ~780K rows (1.3% of 60M).
